title: Commit and resume an approved batch
preconditions: The manifest revision is immutable, approved, fully reconciled, compatible, owner-only, and contains stable LEDGER idempotency identities for all accepted candidates.
postconditions: Every candidate has one durable terminal or explicitly unresolved receipt outcome; repeated resume creates no second canonical transaction; the original source remains unchanged.
main_flow: 1. INGEST validates the exact manifest, approval, adapter version, and discovered LEDGER contract before mutation. 2. For each accepted candidate, INGEST invokes only the public LEDGER transaction command with the recorded idempotency identity. 3. INGEST durably records each structured accepted, exact-duplicate, conflict, or unresolved outcome in the Import Receipt. 4. If execution is interrupted, the owner invokes resume for the same manifest. 5. Resume replays only unresolved candidates with their original identity/request; LEDGER returns prior results for already committed candidates. 6. INGEST returns a complete receipt whose canonical transaction references round-trip from LEDGER.
alt_flows: 1a. Manifest integrity, approval, adapter version, or LEDGER contract validation fails -> no new mutation
2a. LEDGER returns a validation or identity conflict -> record the stable candidate outcome and stop before unsafe continuation
3a. Receipt persistence fails -> stop further candidates and preserve the last durable resume point
4a. Process ends after LEDGER commit but before receipt update -> same-identity replay recovers the original result
5a. Retry request differs materially from the original -> preserve the original LEDGER effect and return stable conflict
metadata: {"depth_tier":3,"primary_actor":"P1","trigger":"The Personal Finance Owner explicitly requests commit of an approved manifest revision.","status":"draft"}
